items.title: Era Adjustment Across Years
content: In 2026, we observed a massive officiating shift (FT rate surged from ~0.267 to 0.315), adding over 3 free throw attempts per team per game. This deep dive extends that analysis across all available years in our database to build a complete era-adjustment map.
When backtesting our models on historical data, we need to adjust player stat projections based on the era they played in.

We should incorporate a `season_ft_rate_modifier` into the core modeling pipeline to normalize all historical scoring to the current 2026 environment. Without this, our models will systematically over-project points for historical games, appearing "wrong" when they are actually just calibrated to a higher-scoring era.

items.title: Role-Conditional Minutes (Slot-Delta)
content: We tested whether replacing a player's raw rolling average of minutes with a "slot-delta" approach would yield a more accurate minutes-played projection. The hypothesis was that when a starter is out, the bench player inheriting their slot should receive a minutes bump proportional to that role.
However, after running a full backtest on the 2024 and 2025 seasons, we proved this to be **noise**.

The slot-delta model consistently performed worse than a simple exponentially weighted moving average (EWMA). By attempting to precisely map out who takes whose minutes when injuries occur, the model overfit to noisy rotation data and introduced more variance than the naive baseline.
The baseline v8 (EWMA) model remains our core, providing a more stable and accurate foundation for player projections. Sometimes, trying to be too clever with the data just introduces noise.

placeholder: We are currently investigating new ideas.
